note_data.title: Diseases, Causes and Prevention
keyPoints: Disease is defined as any disturbance in the structure and function of the body or mind
Diseases are classified into two main types: communicable (infectious) and non-communicable (non-infectious)
Communicable diseases are caused by pathogens like bacteria, viruses, fungi and parasites that spread from one person to another
Non-communicable diseases are not spread from person to person and include genetic disorders, deficiency diseases and degenerative diseases
Prevention is better than cure - vaccination, proper hygiene and balanced diet help prevent diseases
Common communicable diseases in Pakistan include tuberculosis, hepatitis, malaria, dengue fever and cholera
AIDS is caused by HIV virus which destroys the immune system making body vulnerable to infections
Deficiency diseases like rickets (Vitamin D), scurvy (Vitamin C) and beriberi (Vitamin B1) can be prevented by balanced diet
Personal hygiene, environmental sanitation and safe drinking water are essential for disease prevention

definitions.definition: Disease is any disturbance in the structure and function of the body or mind that results in physical or mental disorder
A disease that can be transmitted from one person to another through various means is called communicable disease
A disease-causing organism such as bacteria, virus, fungi or parasite is called a pathogen
The ability of the body to resist and fight against diseases is called immunity
The process of introducing weakened or dead pathogens into the body to develop immunity against specific diseases is called vaccination

importantQuestions.question: Define disease and write two examples of communicable diseases.
importantQuestions.answer: Disease is any disturbance in the structure and function of the body or mind. Examples: Tuberculosis, Malaria.

importantQuestions.question: Explain the causes and prevention of AIDS.
importantQuestions.answer: AIDS is caused by HIV virus. Causes: unprotected sexual contact, contaminated blood transfusion, sharing infected needles, mother to child transmission. Prevention: safe sexual practices, screening blood before transfusion, avoiding sharing of needles, awareness campaigns, proper sterilization of medical instruments.

mcqs.question: Which disease is caused by deficiency of Vitamin C?
Which of the following is a non-communicable disease?

commonMistakes: Students often confuse communicable with non-communicable diseases
Mixing up deficiency diseases with their respective vitamins
Writing incomplete definitions - always include both structure and function disturbance for disease definition
Forgetting to mention prevention methods when asked about disease prevention
Confusing pathogens - remember bacteria, viruses, fungi and parasites are all different types
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question about disease types in every BSEK paper
AIDS prevention methods are frequently asked in long questions worth 5-8 marks
Memorize exact definitions as they appear in STBB textbook
Classification of diseases diagram appears regularly - practice drawing it
Deficiency diseases and their vitamin sources are common MCQ topics
note_data.keyDiagram: Classification of Diseases flowchart showing Disease branching into Communicable (caused by pathogens: bacteria, virus, fungi, parasite) and Non-communicable (genetic disorders, deficiency diseases, degenerative diseases)
